search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae gastroenteritis adults diarrhea telehealth clinical guideline PDF
site:nice.org.uk diarrhea adults red flags guideline
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Antimicrobial selection and dosing were outside this documentation-only mapping.
Pediatric evidence was not applied to an unrestricted workflow.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adults with acute gastroenteritis; the legacy diarrhea workflow has no adult or acute-duration filter.
setting_applicability: Partial: direct for DHA-licensed telehealth acute gastroenteritis assessment and referral, not chronic diarrhea or every in-person context.
UAE_applicability: Direct Dubai DHA guidance for the mapped adult telehealth fields.
recency_verification: The current DHA Version 2 Issue 2 adult gastroenteritis guideline was verified as the official copy and remains within its 2029 revision period.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult acute diarrhea and gastroenteritis presentation terms within DHA telehealth.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines adult gastroenteritis use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped duration, frequency, consistency, blood or mucus, fever, vomiting, exposure, hydration, medicine history, presets, and structured history.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines acute diarrheal illness and records stool frequency, duration, nausea, vomiting, fever, abdominal pain, exposure pattern, and dehydration features.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped blood, dehydration, fever, nocturnal symptoms, hydration assessment, and assessed-negative fields as prompts only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists shock, severe dehydration, intractable vomiting, severe abdominal pain, prolonged symptoms, older age, comorbidity, pregnancy, antibiotic exposure, nocturnal diarrhea, and bloody or black stool.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed stool and blood investigation fields in qualified complicated or nonviral contexts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes history-based assessment and investigation for suspected nonviral or complicated gastroenteritis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped safety, clinician referral, and worsening follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.

unresolved_source_gaps: Children, chronic diarrhea, and non-telehealth settings are outside the exact source.

Persistent diarrhea over 6 weeks, weight loss, family history of IBD, broad abdominal examination or tenderness, previous records, calprotectin, generic clinician plan, fixed follow-up ranges, PRN wording, and some follow-up fields remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, infection attribution, investigation selection or interpretation, treatment, antimicrobial choice, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.

legacy_item_support_mappings.direct_relationship: The exact DHA adult telehealth scope supports these acute diarrhea presentation and matching terms; chronic and pediatric use remain outside scope.
